# Questions in Livestream 135

Live Stream #135: Full Time!!! Full Time!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:48</a> | In Ezekiel 1:16, is the passage describing cherubim or thrones? | He said it is more than a plain throne, perhaps cherubim or something cherubim-like, but the text does not let him identify it absolutely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=1146" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:06</a> | What should people do if they struggle with lust when reading non-PG biblical passages such as Song of Solomon or Genesis 19? | He treated Matthew 5 seriously and framed the issue around the heart, oath faithfulness, and self-control rather than a simplistic avoidance rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=1968" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:48</a> | Was the Tower of Babel a historical or prehistorical event? | He said it is difficult because Babel was likely a ziggurat-like event and the chronology is not simple to pin down. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=2136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:36</a> | Who is redeemed in Exodus 13:15, Exodus 34:20, and Numbers 3:46-49, and why does the number of Levites in the census relate to the firstborn? | He distinguished several kinds of redemption: Passover redemption from judgment, ongoing firstborn redemption, a head-tax redemption, and a Numbers 3 redemption tied to clan increase and excess slots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=2782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:22</a> | Were there really Jewish temples in Egypt at Elephantine and Leontopolis, and what is known about them? | He said there were three temples of Yahweh in Egypt, including Alexandria, Elephantine, and Tell el-Yehudiyeh/Leontopolis, with the latter two leaving more usable evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=3257" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:17</a> | Do angels image God? | Probably not. He argued that imaging God belongs to embodied humanity, while angels are a different category of spiritual beings with their own attributes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=3566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:26</a> | Has he read the Bhagavad Gita, and what is his opinion of its philosophy? | He had not read it yet; he said other religious texts, especially the Quran, were higher on his reading priority list. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=3924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:24</a> | How should Leviticus 20:21 and 18:16 be reconciled with Deuteronomy 25:5-6 on marrying a brother's wife? | He treated the laws as case-law-like rulings with exceptions, with levirate marriage as a specific exceptional case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=4536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:36</a> | Is it reasonable to think Sodom and Gomorrah were historical if they have not been found, and would their tells be visible? | He said not necessarily; archaeology does not guarantee that every ancient city will survive as an obvious tell. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=5201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:41</a> | What is his favorite fictional franchise, whether comics, movies, games, or another medium? | He named different favorites by medium, including Gundam for manga/anime, Fallout and Bethesda games, and Babylon 5 for television science fiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=5630" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:50</a> | Why did Israel make a calf in Exodus 32:4 instead of another image? | He said bovine worship was widespread across the ancient Near East, so the calf fit a default religious pattern familiar from Egypt and Canaanite/Baal symbolism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=6149" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:29</a> | What is heaven, and what would it look like? | He described heaven as abundant life, restored relationships, activity, joy, and creation made new, not a static cloud-and-harp image. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=6476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:56</a> | What is the Jewish view of heaven, if there is one? | He said there is no single Jewish view and deferred to more qualified Jewish voices, noting views differ by community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=6681" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:21</a> | Since sayings about some not tasting death are followed by the Transfiguration, can we say they refer to that event? | He examined the synoptic passages and treated the Transfiguration connection as important, while still noting interpretive complexity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=7024" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:04</a> | What are his thoughts on evolution, and are there better options? | He stayed out of the evolution/creation debate, saying he sees problems with multiple origin models and treats the issue as secondary to affirming that God created. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=7560" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:00</a> | What are his thoughts on using critical analysis in history? | He said the question was unclear because "critical analysis" could mean several things, so he could not answer it well without a clearer definition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=7709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:29</a> | Are there books he recommends on the nature of angelic beings and demons? | The transcript moves into a long answer on angelic beings; he treats the topic as complex and tied to biblical and theological categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=8058" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:18</a> | Is defining sola scriptura as the Bible being the sole infallible authority for faith and practice meaningfully different from saying it is the final authority? | He said the views often agree on the Bible as the sole infallible authority, but differ in how other authorities are consulted and ordered under Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=8362" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:22</a> | Has he ever thought of making a reading list on the Bible and Egypt? | He noted that a reading list already exists or has effectively been done, so he did not need to make one from scratch. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=8992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:52</a> | Are occultists such as Crowley, Order of the Golden Dragon, or Hermetics performing magic in the correct Egyptian way? | No. He said those groups are not practicing Egyptian rituals the way ancient Egyptians did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=9222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:42</a> | Are we in the end times for traditionalists? | He said "end times" depends on definition, warned against overreading ordinary events as signs, and emphasized faithfulness over date-setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=9747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:27</a> | What impact do secret societies have on Christianity and the modern worship of progress? | He thought their direct impact is limited because they are secret and exclusive, and said modern cultural and ethical currents are a bigger issue for Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=10032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:12</a> | What is his favorite book in the Pentateuch, and why? | He named Exodus first because it is where he has done the most work, but also praised Numbers as an underrated and fascinating book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=10250" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:50</a> | When will there be a new coffee mug, perhaps one with Moses kicking a big rock? | He said they are considering more merchandise, including possible new mug ideas, but had not made a firm decision or release date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=10573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:13</a> | What is his favorite book in the Prophets, and why? | He chose Isaiah for its history, prophecy, poetry, theology, and messianic richness, while also saying he appreciates Jeremiah's critique of false prophets. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=10777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:37</a> | What is his favorite book in the Writings? | He identified Ecclesiastes, praising its "under the sun" perspective, poetry, and how it asks important questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=11100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:00</a> | What does he think about Christian Marxism? | He said he is not a fan, mainly because of Marxism rather than because Christians are discussing it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=11305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:25</a> | What are his thoughts on using the Kuzari argument and national experiential tradition as an inductive argument for textual traditions? | He thought the argument becomes tenuous, especially when pressed into textual-tradition claims. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=11711" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:11</a> | What are his thoughts on estrie, Jewish female vampires? | He had not heard of them before, but found the topic intriguing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=12207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:27</a> | Did Jews ever abuse the Jubilee by waiting until afterward to incur debts and get slaves, or did the market adjust to the years left? | He said both happened: market values adjusted as release years approached, and some Israelites also gamed or ignored the system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=12449" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:29</a> | Was "today this is fulfilled in your hearing" the greatest mic-drop moment of all time? | He agreed it was a major moment and used it to praise Isaiah's prophetic and theological depth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=12584" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:44</a> | How much does Philo of Alexandria have in common with ancient Israelites in interpreting Scripture, and did they use allegory similarly? | He said Philo blends Jewish interpretation with Greek philosophical approaches, including allegory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=12808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:28</a> | Would that distinction apply to the Jerusalem and Babylonian Talmuds? | Yes, there would be a difference, though the identity and framing of the Jerusalem Talmud is itself complicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=13090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:10</a> | Can "pillar of salt" in Genesis 19:26 be rendered as "pillar of powder," perhaps from vaporization by extreme heat? | He said the lexical range points to salt, not powder or ash, so "powder" would not be a good rendering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=13513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:13</a> | Is there an ancient Near Eastern context for being turned into salt? | He connected the image to the Dead Sea region, its salts, and local geography, while treating the explanation cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=13820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:20</a> | Could the plagues have started around the beginning of the Egyptian year during the five days when disease was greatly feared? | He said the Egyptian calendar was not yet intercalated at the Exodus period, so those days would not line up with September 11 and were festival days rather than plague-fear days. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=14110" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:10</a> | Why do some translate "the angel of the Lord" as "the presence of the Lord"; is that a way to avoid something? | He called it a theological or idiom-explaining translation choice rather than a literal rendering of "angel" or "messenger." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/e4ZN0Zc9I7Y?t=14432" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:32</a> | Do secular universities teach scholarship by Christians generally? | No, not generally. He described the relationship between secular and Christian institutions as complicated and uneven. |
